calendar_system.name: The First Reckoning
calendar_system.note: Arneth's year is longer than Earth's. Characters age one year every 650 in-game days.

periods.name: Deep Night
periods.character: True darkness. The world is still. Most NPCs sleep. Criminals, guards, and the desperate are active.

periods.name: Dawn Glow
periods.character: Sky lightens before the sun clears the horizon. Long, gradual. Farmers and soldiers rise. The asteroid belt fades last.

periods.name: Morning
periods.character: Full daylight. Work begins in earnest. Markets open. Forge fires are up.

periods.name: Midday
periods.character: Peak light and heat. In Scaldas, outdoor labor pauses. In winter, the best warmth of the day.

periods.name: Afternoon
periods.character: The long productive stretch. Most commerce and negotiation happens here. Energy is sustained.

periods.name: Dusk Glow
periods.character: The slow amber and grey fade. Arneth's most distinctive feature. Taverns fill. The asteroid belt becomes visible first, before the stars.

periods.name: Early Night
periods.character: Full dark. Social hours by firelight. The belt dominates. The Scald galaxy visible on clear nights. The world's unguarded hours.

highsun_solaran.note: Dawn Glow begins earlier, Dusk Glow extends. Near-endless summer light.

deepwarden_ashenveil.note: Dawn Glow compressed, Dusk Glow arrives early. The darkness is the dominant experience of deep winter.

days.name: Firstday
days.character: The clean start. Work begins with purpose. The week's contracts and intentions are set.

days.name: Ashday
days.character: Named for the forge ash cleared each morning. The working day with no ceremony — just labor.

days.name: Wayday
days.character: Traditional travel day. Merchants and messengers move between settlements. Roads are busier.

days.name: Thirday
days.character: Purely functional. No ceremony, no tradition. The week's grind.

days.name: Marketday
days.character: Mid-week rest and commerce. Markets open fully. Heavy labor pauses. Community gathering. Temples hold minor observances.

days.name: Hearthday
days.character: Domestic obligations. Repairs, family, home. The day smiths fix their own equipment rather than clients'.

days.name: Sevenday
days.character: The week's middle distance. Neither the energy of the start nor the relief of the end.

days.name: Wardday
days.character: Watch schedules traditionally rotate. Military and civic connotations. The day garrison commanders review their rosters.

days.name: Grayday
days.character: Named for the grey light of late-week fatigue. The grind before rest. Accidents happen on Grayday.

days.name: Restday
days.character: End of week. Major temple observances. Fires kept low. Taverns busy in the evening. The one day most workers are owed.

week.rest_day_notes: Laborers are owed Restday. Marketday depends on employer. Military and watch rotate regardless of rest days.

months.name: Ashenveil
months.character: The ash-grey veil of deep winter sky. Nothing moves willingly. Survival is the only agenda. The coldest sustained period.
months.agricultural: Nothing planted. Nothing harvested. Stores are at their lowest.
months.military: Campaigns impossible except sieges already underway.
months.travel_risk: High — cold, limited light, difficult roads.

months.name: Coldmere
months.character: The cold mere — still frozen water beginning to question itself. False springs come and deceive. The Spring Breaking may arrive in the final week.
months.agricultural: Ice on fields breaking. Preparation for planting begins cautiously.
months.military: Scouts begin moving. No full campaigns.
months.disease_risk: Moderate — winter waning but cold persists.

months.name: Thornmelt
months.character: When the frost leaves the thorns. Thaw makes roads impassable. The Spring Breaking peaks here — most violent weather of the cold half-year. Then sudden explosive growth.
months.agricultural: Ground thawing. Plowing begins after Breaking passes. Flood risk in river valleys.
months.military: Impassable roads prevent campaign movement. Siege warfare may resume.
months.disease_risk: Moderate — thaw exposes winter's accumulated filth.
months.travel_risk: Very high during Breaking. Moderate after.

months.name: Sowing
months.character: Planting begins. The year's work starts in earnest. Energy, purpose, cautious optimism. The Furrow constellation rises at dusk — the Reclaimed Path considers this the true new year.
months.agricultural: Primary planting month. Most important agricultural labor of the year.
months.military: Campaign season begins. Armies can move.

months.name: Greenmantle
months.character: The green mantle spreading over the land. Everything pushing upward. The year's most energetic month. Recruitment peaks — people willing to take risks.
months.agricultural: Crops established. Secondary planting. Livestock moving to pasture.
months.military: Full campaign season. Best conditions for movement.

months.name: Solaran
months.character: The sun's month. Long days — up to 19 hours of usable light. Heat building. The Scald galaxy first becomes clearly visible on clear nights. Campaign armies are fully deployed.
months.agricultural: Growth phase. Minimal labor needed. Livestock at full summer pasture.
months.military: Peak campaign month. Armies well-supplied, roads perfect.
months.disease_risk: Low early, rising late as heat builds.

months.name: Scaldas
months.character: The burning month. Heat at its peak. Drought risk builds. Water sources require careful management. The Autumn Breaking arrives in the final week — the violent collision of summer and autumn.
months.agricultural: Drought monitoring critical. Late grain ripening.
months.military: Campaigns continue but heat takes toll on troops and horses.
months.disease_risk: High — water contamination in heat. The deadliest disease month.
months.travel_risk: Low physically, but heat exhaustion risk on long journeys.

months.name: Reapfall
months.character: The reaping fall — harvest and the coming turn. The most critical month of the year. A failed harvest here determines who dies in winter. The Autumn Breaking peaks in early Reapfall — tornadoes, hailstorms, violent thunderstorms. Then the harvest race begins.
months.agricultural: THE critical month. All hands harvest. A hailstorm now is a catastrophe.
months.military: Campaigns wind down — armies need to return before harvest fails without them.

months.name: Goldenhold
months.character: When the gold of harvest is held in the cellar. Preservation work in every home. Markets at their peak — the last flush of abundance. The Autumn Meteor Shower peaks mid-month. Political negotiations before winter locks movement.
months.agricultural: Preservation, storage, secondary harvest. Root vegetables.
months.military: Final campaign settlements. Nobody wants to winter in the field.
months.travel_risk: Low — best travel month of the year.
months.special_event: Autumn Meteor Shower — considered auspicious. Peak asteroid belt activity.

months.name: Duskarn
months.character: The darkening — the year descending. Days shortening noticeably. Urgency setting in. NPCs are measurably more transactional, more anxious. The last comfortable travel month.
months.agricultural: Final preparations. Root cellars sealed. Firewood stocked.

months.name: Harrowfrost
months.character: The harrowing frost — the first killing cold. Preparation ends whether you are ready or not. The last stragglers on the roads. Whatever you have in store is what you have.

months.name: Sternveld
months.character: The severe field — unyielding, iron-hard ground. Full winter. The forge season for smiths. Indoor work, steady demand. Communities locked in with each other. Tensions build.

months.name: Deepwarden
months.character: The deep warden — winter at its most vigilant and hostile. The worst of it. Food stores at critical levels in bad years. Ashenveil follows. The Winter Meteor Shower peaks mid-month — considered an omen of a hard season ahead.
months.disease_risk: Very high — peak winter mortality month.
months.special_event: Winter Meteor Shower (the Winter Sparks) — considered ominous. Asteroid belt activity.

seasons.name: Winter
seasons.character: Cold, dark, survival. The longest season. Food from stores. Military action stops. Disease peaks. Communities locked together.
simulation_effects: Food consumption from stores — tracked daily
Travel speed reduced 40% without winter preparation
Disease probability elevated
NPC morale declines week by week if food stores are low
Forge and indoor craft productivity at yearly peak

seasons.name: The Spring Breaking
seasons.character: Winter's last violence meeting the warming ground. The most dangerous travel period of the year. Unpredictable. Cannot be planned around — it happens to you.
weather_events: Rapid temperature swings within a single day
simulation_effects: Road travel: 60% speed reduction during active Breaking weather
River flood risk: elevated — tracked per river system
Crop planting: cannot begin until Breaking passes
Varathi ruin sites: violent rains expose previously buried sites — Salvage Authority schedules around this deliberately
Morale effects: thundersnow events generate NPC omen-talk for weeks

cultural_notes.moor_communion: The Spring Breaking is a sacred period in the Free Fens — the boundary between living and dead considered thinnest during storm events
cultural_notes.salvage_authority: Deliberately schedules excavation to begin immediately after Spring Breaking — exposed ruin sites are the most productive salvage opportunity
cultural_notes.common_people: A mild Spring Breaking is spoken of with relief for years afterward

seasons.name: Spring
seasons.character: Growth, energy, movement. The year's most optimistic season. Recruitment peaks. Campaign season begins.
simulation_effects: Agricultural production begins — tracked per farm/region
Trade route activity increases significantly
Military recruitment elevated
NPC morale improves week by week

seasons.name: Summer
seasons.character: Heat, campaigns, drought risk. Peak military activity. Peak disease risk in the final weeks of Scaldas. The Scald galaxy most visible.
simulation_effects: Drought index begins accumulating if rainfall below threshold
Disease probability peaks in late Scaldas
Military campaign effectiveness at maximum
Trade at yearly peak

seasons.name: The Autumn Breaking
seasons.character: Summer heat colliding with first cold fronts. Most violent weather of the warm half-year. Hailstorms during Reapfall harvest can destroy a standing crop in an hour.
weather_events: Tornadoes — more frequent than Spring Breaking due to heat differential
simulation_effects: Harvest destruction risk: hailstorm during active harvest = partial to total crop loss
Trade caravans: significant disruption
Military: campaigns forced to pause or end
NPC anxiety: elevated — harvest at risk

cultural_notes.scorched_covenant: Considers Autumn Breaking's first major lightning storm the true beginning of their liturgical year
cultural_notes.sailors: Call it 'the Argument' — the sea and sky fighting, ships caught in the middle
cultural_notes.farmers: The most feared weather period. A bad Autumn Breaking in a drought year is a food crisis

seasons.name: Autumn
seasons.character: Harvest, preservation, urgency. The most politically active season — everything must be settled before winter locks movement.
simulation_effects: Harvest resolution: final yield calculated from growing season + Breaking damage + drought index
Food prices peak then fall as harvest comes in
Political negotiations elevated — lords settle disputes before winter
Trade at second yearly peak in Goldenhold

seasons.name: Early Winter
seasons.character: The closing down. Whatever you have prepared is what you have. The long siege of winter begins.
simulation_effects: Final trade caravans of the year
Store levels locked in — winter survival math begins
Smith and indoor craft trade increases
NPC movement decreases to settlement-level

hud_display.weather_line: Season: Autumn  |  Weather: Overcast, wind from the northwest, cooling  |  Region: Thornsveld Marches
fields: Week number of month (Week X of 5)
Current weather (updated daily, more frequently during Breaking)
hud_display.weather_display_note: Shows current conditions and immediate outlook only — not a forecast. What a farmer or sailor would tell you if you asked.
